questions.question: Which State Police launched the ‘Call your Cop’ mobile App, to connect distressed citizens with Police?
questions.explanation: The Nagaland Police recently launched ‘Call your Cop’ mobile App, which will enable all citizens of the state and those in distress to get in touch with the police, in a single click. The features in the Application include directory, alerts, tourist tips, SOS, nearest police station and search.

questions.question: Who is appointed as the chairman of the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC)?
questions.explanation: Senior Indian Revenue Service Officer Shri. Vivek Johri has been appointed as the chairman of the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC), by the personnel ministry. Shri. Vivek Johri is a 1985-batch Indian Revenue Service (Customs and Indirect Taxes) officer who is presently a member of the CBIC.

questions.question: Project Kaveri, which was seen in the news sometime, is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS) sanctioned the indigenous Kaveri engine project in 1989. The project ran for 30 years with an expenditure of ₹2035.56 crore saw the development of nine full prototype engines and four core engines. Recently, it was announced that the Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas is at present integrated with an imported engine, and it is proposed to develop indigenous engines for powering aircraft such as LCA variants and Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) in future.

questions.question: Noney Valley, where the world’s tallest railway bridge pier is being constructed, is located in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: Railways is constructing the world’s tallest railway bridge pier at Noney Valley in the state of Manipur with a height of 141 metres. It is a part of the 111 Kilometre long Jiribam-Imphal railway line, which aims to connect the capital of Manipur with the broad gauge network of the country. The bridge at Noney Valley will surpass the Mala – Rijeka viaduct’s existing record of 139 meters, located in Montenegro, Europe.

questions.question: Alexia Putellas, who won the women’s Ballon d’Or award 2021, belongs to which country?
questions.explanation: Spanish Footballer Alexia Putellas has been awarded the Ballon d’Or Feminin for 2021 during the 2021 Ballon d’Or France Football award. FC Barcelona’s midfielder Alexia Putell is just the third female player to win the prize after Ada Hegerberg and Megan Rapinoe. Argentina’s Lionel Messi has bagged the Ballon d’Or award for a record-extending seventh time. 19-year-old Pedri of Barcelona won the Kopa Trophy for the best player aged under-21.

questions.question: Who is the first President of Barbados, which became the world’s newest republic?
questions.explanation: After 396 years of becoming a British Colony, the Caribbean island country Barbados has recently become the world’s newest republic. The country removed Queen Elizabeth II as the head of the state. Dame Sandra Mason was sworn in as the President by the chief justice of the country. Barbadian singer Rihanna was declared a national hero.

questions.question: How many elephants have been killed in last 10 years, as per the recent data from Environment Ministry?
questions.explanation: After a Tamilnadu-based RTI activist filed an RTI query over the deaths of wild elephants before the Ministry of Environment and Forests’ ‘Project elephant’ division, it was revealed that as many as 1160 wild elephants have been killed in the last 10 years. The elephants were killed due to various reasons such as being hit by trains, electrocution and poaching, across the country in the last decade till December 2020.

questions.question: What is the Global GDP Growth for this year, as forecasted by OECD’s recent latest economic outlook?
questions.explanation: Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development said in its latest economic outlook that global growth is set to hit 5.6% this year. It also highlighted that the Global GDP growth would moderate to 4.5% in 2022 and 3.2% in 2023. That was little changed from a previous forecast of 5.7% for 2021. Due to inflation, major central banks to tighten monetary policy than projected.

questions.question: When is the International Day for the Abolition of Slavery, observed every year by UN?
questions.explanation: The United Nations has adopted 2nd December as the International Day for the Abolition of Slavery. It was on this date, the UNGA passed a resolution in 1949 against Slavery. As per International Labour Organisation, more than 40 million people across the globe are victims of modern slavery, which includes forced labour, debt bondage, forced marriage, and human trafficking.

questions.question: Who has been chosen for “Assam Baibhav” – Assam’s highest civilian award in 2021?
questions.explanation: Indian industrialist and philanthropist Shri. Ratan Tata, has been chosen for “Assam Baibhav” – Assam’s highest civilian award, for his exceptional contribution towards cancer care in the state. He was instrumental in constructing 16 hospitals under the Assam Cancer Care Foundation, of which 10 hospitals will be completed by this year and operationalised in April 2022.
